---
title: "Practice: Ordered Bars"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Goal

Practice the basic workflow from the counts and ordering module:

1. load data
2. reorder a categorical variable
3. plot ordered bars

# Data

Use:

`data/real/state_population_ranks.csv`

# Task

Create an ordered bar chart of state population.

Keep it simple. You do not need to use every state if that makes the chart crowded.

# Work

## Load Data

```{r}
# Load the state population data.

dat <- readr::read_csv("data/real/state_population_ranks.csv")
```

## Reorder

```{r}
# Pick a set of states and reorder state_name by population.

states_for_plot <- dat |>
  filter(...) |>
  mutate(
    state_name = reorder(..., ...)
  )
```

## Plot Bars

```{r}
# Make an ordered bar chart.

ggplot(
  states_for_plot,
  aes(x = ..., y = ...)
) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "...",
    x = NULL,
    y = "..."
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
